name: superman push-up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
As you lower your body, simultaneously lift your right arm and left leg off the ground, extending them straight out.
Pause for a moment at the top, then lower your arm and leg back down while pushing yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, this time lifting your left arm and right leg.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

As you lower your body, simultaneously lift your right arm and left leg off the ground, extending them straight out. Pause for a moment at the top, then lower your arm and leg back down while pushing yourself back up to the starting position. Repeat the movement, this time lifting your left arm and right leg. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
